tool_args.label: Fordham Road – Inwood Bus Priority - Community Board 6 Transportation Committee – June 22, 2023

payload.title: Fordham Road – Inwood Bus Priority
payload.description: Presentation to Bronx Community Board 6 Transportation Committee on Fordham Road bus priority alternatives, outreach timeline, ABLE camera evaluation, and traffic analysis

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

tool_args.label: MTA
NYC Department of Transportation (NYC DOT)

payload.entity_name: New York City Department of Transportation

tool_args.label: BETTERBUSES
Bronx Community Board 6
Fordham Road – Inwood Bus Priority

payload.description: Study of three alternatives for bus priority treatments along Fordham Road: Alternative A (Offset Bus Lanes), Alternative B (Eastbound Busway), Alternative C (Two-Way Busway). Presented to Bronx Community Board 6 in June 2023.

tool_args.label: Fordham Road

payload.description: Critical crosstown transportation corridor serving as a major Bronx-Manhattan connection. Vision Zero Priority Corridor. 85,000 average daily bus riders on 5 routes.

tool_args.label: Bx12 SBS

payload.description: First SBS route launched in 2008. Busiest bus route in the Bronx, second busiest in NYC after the M15. 37,000 daily ridership.

tool_args.label: Bx12 Local

payload.description: Local bus route on Fordham Road. 5,100 daily ridership.

tool_args.label: Bx9

payload.description: Bus route serving Fordham Road. 22,800 daily ridership.

tool_args.label: Bx17

payload.description: Bus route serving Fordham Road. 7,600 daily ridership.

tool_args.label: Bx22

payload.description: Bus route serving Fordham Road. 12,300 daily ridership.

tool_args.label: Existing Curbside Bus Lanes on Fordham Road

payload.description: Existing curbside bus lanes on Fordham Rd. In effect peak periods, Monday-Friday, 7AM to 7PM, with a two-hour midday window for deliveries. Westbound 7AM to Noon, and 2PM to 7PM. Eastbound 7-10AM, and Noon to 7PM. Segment from Sedgwick Ave to University Ave in effect 7am-7pm Monday-Friday without midday break.

tool_args.label: Alternative A: Offset Bus Lanes in Both Directions

payload.description: Convert existing curbside bus lanes to offset lanes and extend offset lanes. Bus-only lane located one-lane away from curb in effect at all times, 24/7. Single travel lane in both directions. Curbside lane available for pick-up/drop-off & deliveries. Approximately 150+ new curbside parking/loading spaces added to corridor (approximately 50 spaces within CB 6).

tool_args.label: Alternative B: Eastbound Busway from Morris Ave to Webster Ave

payload.location_text: Fordham Road, Morris Ave to Webster Ave, Bronx
payload.description: Eastbound through traffic restrictions on Fordham Rd, except for buses and trucks, with local access on all blocks. Offset lane in westbound direction. Offset bus lanes as proposed in Alternative A outside the Busway section. In effect at all times (24/7).

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road, Morris Ave to Webster Ave, Bronx

tool_args.label: Alternative C: Two-Way Busway between Jerome & Webster Aves

payload.location_text: Fordham Road, Jerome Ave to Webster Ave, Bronx
payload.description: Both eastbound and westbound through traffic restrictions on Fordham Rd, except for buses and trucks, with local access on all blocks. Also includes offset bus lanes proposed in Alternative A outside the Busway section. In effect at all times (24/7).

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road, Jerome Ave to Webster Ave, Bronx

tool_args.label: ABLE On-Bus Camera Enforcement on Bx12 SBS

payload.location_text: Fordham Road between Sedgwick Av & Southern Blvd, Bronx
payload.description: ABLE (Automated Bus Lane Enforcement) implemented on Bx12 SBS on November 18, 2022 with a 60-day warning period. Enforces against vehicles parking or standing in a bus lane during bus lane hours. Two buses must capture same vehicle 5 minutes apart to issue a violation. Nearly 16,000 tickets issued between 11/18/22 and 4/21/23.

normalized_location.raw_text: Fordham Road between Sedgwick Av & Southern Blvd, Bronx

tool_args.label: Fixed-Location Bus Lane Enforcement Cameras on Fordham Road

payload.description: 10 fixed-location bus lane enforcement cameras along Fordham Road. 2 installed in 2011, 8 installed in 2013. 5 cameras in Council District 15 (issued 1,818 violations Nov 2022-Apr 2023). 5 cameras in Council District 14 (issued 2,708 violations Nov 2022-Apr 2023).

tool_args.label: Bx12 SBS speed change in April 2023 since ABLE implementation

payload.description: In April 2023, buses traveled 4.7% faster on Fordham Rd ABLE segments since ABLE implementation in November 2022
payload.scope: Fordham Road between Sedgwick Av & Southern Blvd

tool_args.label: Alternative A selected as best treatment

payload.claim_text: Based on factors identified in the traffic analyses and listening to the community concerns, Alternative A – offset bus lanes along Fordham Rd – is the best treatment to improve overall bus speeds, reliability, and curb demand while minimizing the effects on buses on north/south corridors
payload.subject: Fordham Road Bus Priority project
payload.description: Alternative A (Offset Bus Lanes in Both Directions) selected as the preferred alternative based on traffic analysis and community feedback

tool_args.label: Curb demand challenges on Fordham Road

payload.claim_text: Curbside bus lanes are a less efficient means of balancing bus priority and curb access. Deliveries are only permitted during a 2-hour window in the midday when the bus lanes are not in effect. Expeditious pick-ups and drop-offs by passenger cars are allowed at any time, regardless of bus lane hours, which causes further delays for buses needing to weave in and out of traffic. Observations and data show that existing curbside bus lanes are frequently blocked throughout the day.
payload.subject: Fordham Road existing conditions

tool_args.label: ABLE positive effect but not sufficient alone

payload.claim_text: ABLE bus lane enforcement is having a positive effect on bus speeds on Fordham Road. Enforcement alone has not raised bus speeds to prior levels as blockages remain frequent. Most violations are issued in the central and western parts of Fordham Road. This improved bus lane enforcement, paired with improved bus lane designs, can further improve bus speeds for the 85,000 riders per day using Fordham Road.
payload.subject: ABLE evaluation on Fordham Road

tool_args.label: Public support for bus priority on Fordham Road

payload.claim_text: ABNY and Change Research surveyed 302 respondents between November 16–19, 2020. 79% support changes to city streets that can make buses faster and more reliable. 89% support improving existing bus lanes. 66% support additional bus lanes. 70% support a busway. 72% of non-bus riders support improving the existing bus lanes on Fordham Rd.
payload.subject: Public polling on Fordham Road bus priority

tool_args.label: New design adds 150+ parking/loading spaces

payload.claim_text: New design addresses intense curb demand: approximately combined 150+ parking/loading spaces added to corridor instead of confined to two-hour window (approximately 50 spaces within CB 6). Offset bus lanes are not foolproof – double parking can still block the bus lane, but they are an upgrade from curbside bus lanes.
payload.subject: Alternative A offset bus lane design
